title: tRPC auth/authorization middleware distinguishes admin vs editor
description: Add tRPC middleware/procedures that resolve the current user from the session cookie, reject unauthenticated requests on protected routes, and allow role-based gating between `admin` and `editor`.
acceptanceCriteria: A tRPC context function reads the session cookie, looks up the `sessions` row, checks `expira_em` has not passed, and attaches `{ userId, role }` to context (or `null` if absent/expired)
`adminProcedure` throws `FORBIDDEN` if context user's role is not `admin`
Expired sessions are treated as unauthenticated and the stale `sessions` row is deleted on access
Given the confirmed decision that editors have full CRUD on structure (TASK-24), `editorOrAdminProcedure` (or simply `protectedProcedure`) is available for routes both roles can use

steps.details: In `apps/server/src/trpc/context.ts`, parse the `session_id` cookie from the incoming request, query `sessions` joined with `memberships`/`users`, and return `{ userId, role } | null` as part of the tRPC context.
If the session row's `expira_em` is in the past, treat as unauthenticated, and delete the expired row from `sessions` as a side effect of the lookup.

In `apps/server/src/trpc/init.ts`, create `protectedProcedure = publicProcedure.use(({ ctx, next }) => { if (!ctx.user) throw new TRPCError({ code: 'UNAUTHORIZED' }); return next({ ctx: { ...ctx, user: ctx.user } }); })`.
Create `adminProcedure = protectedProcedure.use(({ ctx, next }) => { if (ctx.user.role !== 'admin') throw new TRPCError({ code: 'FORBIDDEN' }); return next(); })`.

Test: request with no cookie on a `protectedProcedure` route returns 401; request with an editor session on an `adminProcedure` route returns 403; request with a valid admin session succeeds; request with an expired session returns 401 and the session row is removed.

technicalNotes: This middleware is the single enforcement point reused by every protected route added in later phases (users, sections, pages, tabs, blocks, previews) — get its shape right before building on top of it.
Deleting expired sessions on access is a lightweight cleanup strategy sufficient for a single-container MVP; a separate cron/sweep job is not needed.
